plan: Query.set_empty() (django/db/models/sql/query.py ~line 1778) only adds a NothingNode to the outer WHERE clause, but for combined queries the compiler branches to get_combinator_sql and builds SQL purely from self.combined_queries, never consulting the outer WHERE, so QuerySet.none() silently returns all union results. Fix set_empty() to propagate emptiness into the combined parts: for query in self.combined_queries: query.set_empty(). This is a backend-agnostic query-construction bug fixed at the single point where the empty state is constructed, so a one-file fix in sql/query.py suffices (ModelMultipleChoiceField.clean and the compiler are consumers, not the bug site).
